app.name: Semrush
app.bestFor: Pairing a full-stack SEO toolkit with an AI Visibility Toolkit that tracks brand citations across ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity and AI Overviews.
app.rankBasis: The only full-suite vendor recurring across all three source roundups, backed by one of the largest SEO review communities and a mature toolkit now extended into AI-search visibility.
app.pricingFree: Limited free account — up to 10 searches/day with restricted reports; no full AI Visibility plan
app.pricingPaid: SEO Toolkit Pro $139.95/mo — 5 projects, 500 tracked keywords and 10,000 results/report
pros: broadest all-in-one SEO workflow in this list
large data set and mature reporting
combines classic SEO with AI visibility
cons: expensive once AI and extra-user add-ons are included
interface and product catalog can feel overwhelming
usage limits remain even on paid plans

app.name: Profound
app.bestFor: Enterprise-grade AI visibility with real user-facing data across 10+ AI engines plus a unique Prompt Volumes metric.
app.rankBasis: A top-two entry across enterprise GEO comparisons, differentiated by prompt-volume data and multi-engine tracking; independent review volume remains smaller than legacy SEO suites.
pros: purpose-built for AI-search measurement
strong enterprise and multi-market reporting
prompt-volume data helps prioritize tracked queries
cons: limited independent review history
less useful for traditional backlink and technical SEO
larger deployments move to custom enterprise pricing

app.name: Ahrefs
app.bestFor: Backlink and keyword depth combined with a Brand Radar for monitoring AI mentions — a classic-SEO powerhouse extending into GEO.
app.rankBasis: Appears in all three independent GEO/AI-visibility sources here and retains category-leading backlink-data mindshare, giving it the strongest classic-SEO adoption signal after Semrush.
app.pricingFree: Ahrefs Webmaster Tools — free for verified sites with limited Site Audit and Site Explorer access
app.pricingPaid: Lite $119/mo — 5 projects, 1,000 reports/user/mo and up to 100,000 total Site Audit crawl pages
features: Brand Radar for AI and web mentions
pros: exceptionally strong backlink and competitive data
fast, polished research workflow
AI visibility sits alongside established SEO data
cons: no general-purpose free plan for competitor research
credit and report limits frustrate some reviewers
AI tracking is newer and less specialized than dedicated GEO tools

app.name: Writesonic
app.bestFor: Pairing GEO monitoring across ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity and Claude with an AI content-generation engine in one platform.
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly shortlisted as the content-plus-GEO option; its large writing-tool user base is a stronger adoption signal than its still-new AI-visibility module.
app.pricingPaid: Starter $99/mo — 50 prompts and 50 answers tracked daily, 1 user and 1 project
features: AI visibility tracking across major engines
pros: connects monitoring directly to content production
covers multiple AI engines in one dashboard
accessible workflow for content teams
cons: no standing free GEO plan; free access is trial-oriented
product packaging changes frequently
reviewers report variable output quality that still needs editing

app.name: SE Ranking
app.bestFor: Embedding AI visibility directly into a full-stack SEO performance suite at a mid-market price.
app.rankBasis: Included by both Semrush and SitePoint as a lower-cost full-suite alternative, with a substantial established SEO review base but less GEO-specific community evidence.
app.pricingPaid: Essential from about $65/mo — 5 projects and 500 keywords tracked daily; 14-day trial only
pros: good feature-to-price ratio for an SEO suite
clear rank-tracking and reporting workflows
more approachable than the largest enterprise suites
cons: no permanent free plan
historical and backlink databases are smaller than Ahrefs or Semrush
AI visibility is an extension rather than the product's core

app.name: Otterly AI
app.bestFor: Affordable, accessible AI-search monitoring for smaller brands, freelancers and agencies.
app.rankBasis: A recurring specialist recommendation in AI-visibility roundups and one of the lowest-cost dedicated trackers, though it has a much smaller review footprint than full SEO suites.
app.pricingPaid: Lite $29/mo — 15 search prompts tracked across 4 AI search engines
pros: low entry price for dedicated GEO monitoring
simple setup and focused reporting
covers the main AI answer engines
cons: 15 prompts is restrictive for multi-product brands
no traditional SEO research toolkit
smaller ecosystem and review base than established suites

app.name: AthenaHQ
app.bestFor: Growth-stage SaaS and mid-market teams wanting a single GEO Score plus an action center of optimization steps.
app.rankBasis: A newer specialist with limited independent coverage; it ranks on its action-oriented GEO workflow and a concrete free entry tier rather than broad market adoption.
app.pricingFree: Essential free plan — $25 in credit, equivalent to 300 credits
app.pricingPaid: Starter $295/mo — 3,600 credits plus add-on credit options
pros: free credits allow a real product evaluation
turns visibility findings into prioritized actions
focuses on brand accuracy as well as mentions
cons: paid entry price is high for smaller teams
thin independent review volume
credit consumption is less intuitive than prompt-based plans

skill.name: claude-seo (Daniel Agrici)
skill.bestFor: The recognized leader among SEO/GEO Agent Skills (~12.2k stars, MIT): 25+ sub-skills and sub-agents covering technical SEO, E-E-A-T, schema, GEO/AEO and local/international SEO with prioritized action plans. Notably pragmatic. Carries strategy and audit checklists — pair with an app-track suite for live rank/backlink data.
skill.rankBasis: With about 12.2k GitHub stars and 1.8k forks, this is the most-adopted dedicated SEO agent-skill repository and remains actively maintained.
features: local, ecommerce and international SEO workflows
pros: unusually comprehensive skill coverage
MIT-licensed and actively maintained
produces structured audits and action plans
cons: live ranking and backlink data require external providers
broad workflow has a learning curve
output quality depends on agent context and source access

skill.name: geo-seo-claude (Zubair Trabzada)
skill.bestFor: A GEO-first SKILL.md bundle (~9.1k stars): citability scoring, AI-crawler / robots.txt analysis for 14+ bots, llms.txt and schema for ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini and AI Overviews. Honest caveat: the repo is overtly promotional (tied to a paid community), so raw stars may overstate maturity.
skill.rankBasis: About 9.1k GitHub stars and 1.5k forks make it the second-largest dedicated GEO skill here, but the repo's promotional tie-in warrants more caution than the raw star count suggests.
pros: deep focus on AI-search optimization
covers major AI crawlers and answer engines
clear deliverables for technical GEO fixes
cons: promotional material is mixed into the repository
real visibility data still needs external checks
less independent validation than the star count implies

skill.name: SerpBear
skill.bestFor: The go-to self-hosted keyword rank tracker (~2.0k stars) — unlimited domains, Google Search Console integration and email alerts. Highest-star standalone OSS app in this set.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 2.0k GitHub stars and 271 forks make SerpBear the highest-adoption standalone open-source rank tracker in this set.
pros: no per-keyword subscription fee
focused, understandable self-hosted UI
strong fit for agencies managing many domains
cons: requires hosting and scheduled-job maintenance
Google scraping needs proxies or a SERP API at scale
does not provide backlink or GEO citation analysis

skill.name: advertools
skill.bestFor: The practitioner reference library for scriptable technical SEO (~1.4k stars): a Python/pandas toolkit with a Scrapy crawler, robots.txt and sitemap parsing, SERP and log analysis. The most mature, respected pro-grade OSS SEO library.
skill.rankBasis: About 1.4k GitHub stars, 247 forks and long-running documentation give it the strongest practitioner-library reputation among scriptable OSS SEO tools here.
features: log, URL and text analysis in pandas
pros: powerful for reproducible analysis in Python
integrates naturally with pandas workflows
mature documentation and broad technical scope
cons: code-first rather than a ready-made dashboard
SERP APIs and infrastructure can cost extra
requires Python and data-analysis competence

skill.name: awesome-seo-tools (SerpApi)
skill.bestFor: A vetted directory of SEO tooling (~1.0k stars) across 10 categories, maintained by SerpApi. A starting point for discovery rather than a tool you run — hence medium.
skill.rankBasis: Its roughly 1.0k GitHub stars and 286 forks are strong for a curated SEO directory, but it ranks below executable tools because it is a reference list rather than software.
features: links to technical and content utilities
pros: useful discovery starting point
maintained by an established search-data vendor
broad coverage without vendor lock-in
cons: not an executable SEO tool
listed projects vary widely in quality and freshness
users must evaluate and assemble their own stack

skill.name: seo-audits-toolkit
skill.bestFor: An all-in-one self-hosted audit app (~802 stars) by Stan Girard (Quivr author): Lighthouse scoring, security headers, SERP rank, and keyword/image/sitemap extraction.
skill.rankBasis: About 802 GitHub stars show meaningful adoption, but the last code push was in 2023 and the 41 open issues make its maintenance signal weaker than newer crawlers.
pros: combines several audit utilities in one app
self-hosted with no usage subscription
covers performance and security alongside SEO
cons: project appears largely inactive since 2023
setup and dependency maintenance fall on the user
less depth than dedicated crawlers and rank trackers

skill.name: LibreCrawl
skill.bestFor: A desktop SEO crawler (~787 stars) positioned as a free Screaming Frog / DeepCrawl alternative — JS rendering, link mapping, Core Web Vitals via PageSpeed, and CSV/JSON/XML export.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 787 GitHub stars and 166 forks show solid early interest in a free desktop crawler, with active 2026 development but a smaller community than mature SEO tools.
features: Core Web Vitals and multi-format export
pros: desktop alternative to subscription crawlers
supports rendered pages and useful exports
active and extensible open-source codebase
cons: newer project with limited long-term track record
desktop resources constrain very large crawls
community support is smaller than commercial crawler ecosystems

skill.name: SEOnaut
skill.bestFor: A mature self-hosted technical-SEO auditor (~741 stars, Go + MySQL) with severity-ranked crawl reports (broken links, redirects, meta issues, heading order) and a dashboard.
skill.rankBasis: About 741 GitHub stars and 122 forks, active 2026 maintenance and a focused technical-audit UI support its place among the smaller self-hosted SEO apps.
pros: clear dashboard for recurring technical audits
lightweight Go-based self-hosting
actionable issue severity rather than raw crawl data
cons: requires database and server administration
no backlink, keyword or AI-visibility database
smaller community and integration ecosystem

skill.name: geo-optimizer-skill (Auriti Labs)
skill.bestFor: An AEO/GEO auditing toolkit (~615 stars) that measures whether ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini and AI Overviews cite your site and generates fixes (robots.txt bot rules, llms.txt, JSON-LD). Despite the name it is a CLI/Python/Astro toolkit, not an MCP server.
skill.rankBasis: About 615 GitHub stars and 76 forks show notable early adoption for a dedicated open-source GEO toolkit, with active development but limited independent review evidence.
features: ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini and AI Overview checks
pros: directly measures GEO outcomes rather than only classic SEO
multiple interfaces suit agents and developers
MIT-licensed and actively maintained
cons: new project with a small independent user base
AI-engine checks may require provider credentials and incur usage cost
results can vary as answer engines change behavior

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt and re-ranked by real quality/reputation/adoption (verified GitHub stars, professional reputation) rather than by form factor — 'kind' is a tag only. It now mixes genuine SKILL.md Agent Skills and notable open-source SEO/GEO repos. Daniel Agrici's claude-seo skill leads (~12.2k stars) as the most-adopted, most-comprehensive SEO Agent Skill. Skills carry strategy and audit checklists; repos (SerpBear, advertools, the crawlers/auditors) do the live crawling, rank tracking and GEO-citation measurement — pair both with an app-track suite for backlink/AI-visibility data. All stars verified via the GitHub API. No MCP servers; the prior MCP entries (Ahrefs/DataForSEO/GSC MCP) were removed as out of scope.
